speaker: interviewer
text: Lillian Bielsky-Bell. December 1, 1996. Mrs. Bielsky, who's in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my brother in 1938 after he finished high school in Bialystok. His name is Mayer Ticktin. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Who is in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my husband, Tovia Bielsky in the Polish Army in 1922.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK, what can you tell us about this?

speaker: interviewee
text: In May 1944.

speaker: interviewer
text: May, 1944. Where was--

speaker: interviewee
text: This was photographed in May, 1944. I personally think it's on the-- on the airport near [? General Platon ?] where the Russian planes used to come.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who are the people in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's a group of our-- Bielsky's unit partisans. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And who is in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's me, Lillian Bielsky-Bell in 1944 after coming out of the woods in Lida.

speaker: interviewer
text: After coming out of--

speaker: interviewee
text: After the liberation.

speaker: interviewer
text: In Lida?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Lida.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, who's in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This photograph is my husband and myself in 1946 coming to Israel.

speaker: interviewer
text: You're living in Israel already.

speaker: interviewee
text: Coming to Israel.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is this on the way or in Israel?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, no, in Israel

speaker: interviewer
text: Is this in Holon?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, I guess so. I don't remember exactly. I think it's in Holon.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you tell us what's in this photo?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's my husband's grave in Jerusalem in [INAUDIBLE]. I don't remember which memorial it was. The second or the-- the second probably.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who are the people in the background?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's me, it's my-- my cousin there, it's-- and two friends.

speaker: interviewer
text: And there's a crowd of people on the side.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, that's right. They-- they all come to the memorial.

speaker: interviewer
text: Were they former partisans as well?

speaker: interviewee
text: They're former partisans of Israel and they live all in Israel. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, can you tell us what this photograph is?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the funeral of my husband, military funeral done by [INAUDIBLE] Israel, and that's the salute for his burial.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was in 1988.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, what is this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This photograph is the memorial service when-- it was a few days after the burial of my husband that we moved him to Israel and my speech to the people.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what occasion is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was the award given to-- to me-- to my husband, which I received in his honor in-- in Holocaust Memorial in Washington, DC on October 1995. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Which event is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was after I received the award for my husband. This is all my nephews and my nieces and my children participating in that event.

speaker: interviewer
text: When was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: That was in October 1995.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who's in the first row? Can you tell us?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the first row is my daughter-in-law, Aviva Bielsky.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is she on the left or the right?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the right. In the middle is Rosie [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] Rosie Herschel. And-- and Sharon Rayn, my granddaughter. On the left is my daughter, Ruth Rayn.

speaker: interviewer
text: Standing?

speaker: interviewee
text: Standing. Near her is her son Brandon Rayn. And then is Robert Bielsky, my younger son. My nephew and niece, Allen Bell. My son, Mickey Bielsky. And my nephew Raymond Herschel and Julie Herschel. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: What event is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was my younger son's wedding, and there is quite a few people that came from Israel to participate, and they're all partisans that joined us in Bielsky's group through all the years of the-- until the liberation.

speaker: interviewer
text: And are you and your husband in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, I'm-- I'm on the right. In the middle is the oldest partisan from our woods, and next to her is my husband.

speaker: interviewer
text: You're wearing the white dress, right?

speaker: interviewee
text: I'm wearing a silver dress. [PAUSES FOR 13 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Which award is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is award at a testimonial dinner that was done from the Federation of Jewish Underground Fighters Against the Nazis in February 17, 1968. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, which award is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was given to us when we came after we left Israel in 1970. It was a big thing done for my husband, a big party. All the partisans got together, and they awarded him with this certificate.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the date?

speaker: interviewee
text: The date was-- I don't know. I know it's 1970, that-- our first time to return to Europe.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: But the date is-- I can only-- I don't--

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. As long as we have the year. OK, which award is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a testimonial dinner for my husband from the Underground Jewish Fighters and Survivors in 1976 in November.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the Americana Hotel.

speaker: interviewer
text: In New York?

speaker: interviewee
text: New York.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Which plaque is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a plaque that was given by Touro College, a testimonial dinner in the-- in the Hilton in New York in 1995, in December 6, five months before he died.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is that 1985?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, 1995. He died in-- oh, I'm sorry, you're right. '85. '86, '86. 1986. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, which award is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the award given for resistance in Washington DC in 1996. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] In October, 1996, I think. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] '95.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, I think it says 1995.

speaker: interviewee
text: You're right. You're right. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, can you tell us about this shirt?

speaker: interviewee
text: This shirt came with my husband together to the woods. He brought it with him. I kept it 55 years as a souvenir. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And that's-- and that's all that I know.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did he wear this when he was living in the woods?

speaker: interviewee
text: Actually, I didn't see him wearing that, but he had it, and he wanted me to keep it as a souvenir all the time, and that's what I did.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK, Mrs. Bielsky, can you tell us who is in your family?

speaker: interviewee
text: On my left is my older son Micky Bielsky. On my right, is my younger son, Robert. On my right is my-- my oldest granddaughter, Sharon Rayn and-- and my daughter-in-law, Chris Bielsky.

speaker: interviewer
text: And the grandchildren?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the first row is Ariele Bielsky, Taylor Bielsky, Tori Bielsky, Sarah Bielsky, and my big boy, Jordan Bielsky.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mrs. Bielsky, what do you have to say to the future generations of your family and all the other families?

speaker: interviewee
text: I would like to-- to tell them that you should always remember their father, what he did, and-- and try to go in-- in his footsteps and be as generous and good as he was. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Thank you very much. Thank you, all of you, for coming and participating. Thank you, Mrs. Bielsky, for sharing your story with us.
